catalog.name: Sultan / Queen Regnant
catalog.summary: The ruling crown office of the Maldives during monarchical periods.

catalog.name: President
catalog.summary: Head of state and government in the republican era.

catalog.name: Furadaana (Furadhaana)
catalog.summary: Royal ministerial council/cabinet around the sovereign, with office composition changing over time.

catalog.name: Kilege
catalog.summary: Senior office in the Furadaana, often described as head of ministers/chief ministerial figure.

catalog.name: Bandeyri
catalog.summary: Court/palace portfolio tied to royal household and palace personnel, with broad state functions in later periods.

catalog.name: Velaanaa
catalog.summary: Revenue-linked office associated with fines, duties, and other fiscal collections in early records.

catalog.name: Faiylia
catalog.summary: Office tied to royal stores and custody/receipt of royal goods.

catalog.name: Dhoshimeynaa
catalog.summary: Military-linked office; described in different periods as army-general/chief defense portfolio.

catalog.name: Hakuraa
catalog.summary: Ministerial office associated with court audience/protocol and, in later descriptions, public works.

catalog.name: Fandiyaaru
catalog.summary: Chief judge/Qadi office in the pre-modern state and monarchy-era judicial structure.

catalog.name: Handeygiri
catalog.summary: High office in later monarchical administration; role descriptions vary from religious portfolio to treasury-central authority.

catalog.name: Faamuladheyri
catalog.summary: Treasury-linked office in descriptions of traditional government portfolios.

catalog.name: Maafaiy
catalog.summary: Revenue office identified as receiver-general in historical descriptions.

catalog.name: Faashanaa
catalog.summary: Police/security portfolio in late monarchical office descriptions.

catalog.name: Naib (Naibu / Al-Naib)
catalog.summary: Deputy/viceroy-style court office title; appears in elite genealogical lines and 19th-century cabinet descriptions.

timeline.label: 14th century (Ibn Battuta era)
timeline.summary: Court records point to a structured cabinet around the vizier and a distinct judicial office (Qadi/Fandiyaaru).

timeline.label: Early 17th century (Pyrard account)
timeline.summary: Furadaana listed with Kilege, Velaanaa, Hakuraa, Fandiyaaru, Dhoshimeynaa, Faiylia, and Bandeyri among principal offices.

timeline.label: 1834 (Christopher account)
timeline.summary: Cabinet descriptions include Kilege, Faiylia, Hakuraa, Bandeyri, Naibu, and Dhoshimeyna as senior posts, with Fandiyaaru still distinct.

timeline.label: 1879 (Bell account)
timeline.summary: A compressed model is reported: three key ministers plus the chief judge; Handeygiri and Faashanaa appear among powerful offices.

timeline.label: 20th century transition
timeline.summary: Constitutional and legal centralization reduced older court-office autonomy; monarchy ended in 1968 and republican executive offices became dominant.

timeline.label: Republican era
timeline.summary: Traditional office titles persist in social memory and elite naming, while formal state leadership is under the presidency and modern institutions.
